commentBody: What you’re all forgetting is the quality of the pork chops. Supermarket species are dreadful; butcher chops are good if the butcher gets his pork from Canada. Once you have it it’s hard to return to lesser grades

I also make a fresh strawberry pie using 7-up or Sprite.  See the www.thegoldendish.com for recipe.

commentBody: I’ve made the same gratin for years from my well worn cookbook “The Secrets of Great French Restaurants. “. Gently bring potatoes cut half inch thick and to a simmer then add heavy cream. Coon until potatoes are somewhat tender but still firm enough. Rub a gratin dishwith cut clove of garlic and butter. Add potatoes and milk mixture. Season with salt and pepper and nutmeg. Add grated Gruyère and bake until crusty and bubbly. Delicious. Great with roast lamb and  vs if peas

commentBody: Now that I’ve had slice after slice each time  I pass by the cake plate in which it rests ( day 2 ) each bite is a revelation. If you don’t have superfine that’s ok. As for “raw” pecans I’m not sure what those are except I searched for them at the supermarket and found a a box that said raw. I only use local farm eggs (@$7/dozen )  I get especially for my baking and and barely have any left. I’m not sure if I’ll make it again but relish every leftover morsel.

author: Emily Weinstein
recipeTitle: Pistachio Pound Cake With Strawberries Recipe
commentBody: I'd like to know how the Times calculates the conversion of weights from cups to grams.  In this recipe we're instructed to use 195 grams all-purpose flour for the equivalent of 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour.  By most conversion charts 1 1/2 cups should be anywhere from 180 to 187 grams flour to equal 1 1/2 cups.  King Arthur Flour's conversion chart says that 1 cup all-purpose flour  is equal to 120 grams.  This diversion has been in many Times recipes. What gives?

author: Eric Kim
commentBody: I roast a whole chicken often for my household of two. With leftovers I’ll either make chicken enchiladas or a variation of chicken a la king or creamed chicken served over biscuits or cornbread. Pimentos are essential for a spike of color and you should try to use homemade chicken stock instead of canned. It’s always a very pleasing dish, a nice reprieve from all the miso spiked or hot sauce concoctions that plague our culinary hedonism.

recipeTitle: Spiced Pea Stew With Yogurt Recipe
commentBody: My favorite dish of frozen peas is mousseline of  peas, a recipe from a cookbook on French cooking that I’ve been making for years. I serve it with roast lamb and daupininois of potatoes. Cook the peas in plenty of water with a small carrot, peeled onion and a sprig or two of thyme. Get them real soft. Drain.  Discard the carrot, onion and thyme. Put in a food processor or food mill. Purée, adding generous bits of butter and heavy cream until the puree is smooth. Absolutely delicious

author: Claire Saffitz
commentBody: Full fat buttermilk is a misnomer. It means that it’s pure buttermilk made from cream with little flecks of butter in the milk. It has no cultures and has a short shelf life. I used to drive 50 miles to an old fashioned dairy farm with the most intense raw butter, milk and cream and their buttery butter milk. Generally I use Kate’s buttermilk available in most stores in Maine. I think it fits term full fat an item used in the south
